questions.stem:
—I can't find my key. ______ you ______ it anywhere?
—Yes. I saw it on the sofa half an hour ago, but I'm not sure if it is still there.
questions.explanation: 这道题考查现在完成时和一般过去时的区别。现在完成时(Have you seen...)强调过去动作对现在的影响（钥匙现在找不到，问是否见过，与现在有关）；而一般过去时(Did you see...)只强调过去某个具体时间发生的动作。答句中虽然提到了half an hour ago，但问句关心的是"到目前为止是否见过"，与现在找不到钥匙的情况相关，因此用现在完成时。解题技巧：看到与现在有联系的语境（找不到钥匙），优先考虑现在完成时。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The girl ______ won the first prize in the English speech contest is from Class 3.
questions.explanation: 这道题考查定语从句关系代词的选择。先行词是The girl（指人），且在从句中作主语（won the first prize），因此用who。which用于指物；whom用于指人但在从句中作宾语；whose表示所属关系（...的）。从句中缺少主语，所以选who。解题口诀：指人主格用who，宾格whom，所属whose；指物用which，that可替人替物。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: These old houses ______ by the government next year to make room for a new park.
questions.explanation: 这道题考查一般将来时的被动语态。主语These old houses（旧房子）是动作pull down（拆除）的承受者，因此要用被动语态；时间状语next year表示将来，所以用一般将来时的被动语态will be pulled down。选项A是主动语态，C是现在进行时主动语态，D是一般过去时主动语态，均不符合。解题技巧：看到by+动作执行者，立即考虑被动语态；结构为will be done。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
—Could you please tell me ______?
—Sure. It's about 10 minutes' walk from here.
options: A. how far is the museum
B. how far the museum is
D. how can I get to the museum
questions.explanation: 这道题考查宾语从句的语序。宾语从句必须使用陈述句语序（主语+谓语），而不能用疑问句语序。选项A、C、D都是疑问句语序（is the museum, is the museum, can I），只有B是陈述句语序（the museum is）。根据答句"步行约10分钟"，问的是距离，用how far。解题技巧：宾语从句三要素：连接词+陈述语序+时态呼应。检查方法：把从句部分单独拿出来看是否是完整陈述句。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The math problem was so challenging that only three students in our class worked it out. The underlined word "challenging" probably means ______.
questions.explanation: 这道题考查通过上下文猜测词义。关键线索是后半句"only three students... worked it out（只有三名学生做出来）"，说明这道题很难。challenging意为"有挑战性的"，即困难且考验人的。选项A（简单有趣）和D（简短简单）与语境相反；C（无聊冗长）没有体现。解题技巧：利用因果关系（so...that...）和对比关系（only three students暗示难度）来猜测词义。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: We should ______ our old books and clothes to the children in poor areas instead of throwing them away.
questions.explanation: 这道题考查动词短语辨析。give away意为"捐赠，赠送"；give up意为"放弃"；give in意为"屈服，让步"；give out意为"分发，耗尽"。根据句意"把旧书旧衣服____给贫困地区的孩子们"，应该是"捐赠"的意思。解题技巧：记住常见give短语的核心含义：away（离开/送走）→赠送；up（向上/结束）→放弃；in（进入）→屈服；out（出去）→分发。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: If you add the prefix "im-" to the word "possible", you get a word meaning ______.
questions.explanation: 这道题考查前缀的构词法。im-是否定前缀，加在possible（可能的）前面构成impossible，意为"不可能的"。选项A意为"非常可能"，C是副词"可能地"，D意为"充满可能性"。解题技巧：初中常见否定前缀：un-（happy→unhappy）、dis-（like→dislike）、im-（polite→impolite）、in-（correct→incorrect）、ir-（regular→irregular）。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: 这道题考查逻辑关系和语境理解。根据下文"Another tip"（另一个建议）和文章结构，这里应该是第一个建议，所以用First（首先）。Finally表示最后，However表示转折，Therefore表示结果，均不符合此处列举第一条建议的语境。解题技巧：关注文章结构信号词，如first, then, next, finally等表示顺序；also, another表示并列补充。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a green project called "Zero Waste Classroom". The project aims to help students understand the importance of environmental protection.

(8)_______, we were asked to bring reusable bottles instead of plastic ones. At first, many students found it (9)_______ to remember bringing their bottles every day. However, after two weeks, it became a good habit.

The most exciting part was the recycling competition. Each class had to collect waste paper and bottles. The class (10)_______ collected the most would win a prize. My classmates and I worked hard together. We put a special box at the back of the classroom and encouraged everyone to use it.

By the end of the month, we were surprised to find that we had produced 60% less waste than before. This experience taught us that small actions can make a big (11)_______. 

Now, the "Zero Waste" idea has spread to other schools in our city. We feel proud that we are helping to create a better (12)_______ for future generations.

questions.explanation: 这道题考查形容词辨析和语境推理。根据下文"However, after two weeks, it became a good habit（然而两周后成了好习惯）"的转折关系，可以推断起初应该是"困难的"。如果一开始就很easy（容易），就不需要However转折了。interesting（有趣的）和relaxing（放松的）也不符合语境。解题技巧：利用转折词However判断前后语义相反，后面是"became a good habit"，前面应该是"不容易/困难"。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a green project called "Zero Waste Classroom". The project aims to help students understand the importance of environmental protection.

(8)_______, we were asked to bring reusable bottles instead of plastic ones. At first, many students found it (9)_______ to remember bringing their bottles every day. However, after two weeks, it became a good habit.

The most exciting part was the recycling competition. Each class had to collect waste paper and bottles. The class (10)_______ collected the most would win a prize. My classmates and I worked hard together. We put a special box at the back of the classroom and encouraged everyone to use it.

By the end of the month, we were surprised to find that we had produced 60% less waste than before. This experience taught us that small actions can make a big (11)_______. 

Now, the "Zero Waste" idea has spread to other schools in our city. We feel proud that we are helping to create a better (12)_______ for future generations.

questions.explanation: 这道题考查定语从句关系代词。先行词是The class（班级），指物，后面从句collected the most缺少主语。虽然which也可以指物，但先行词被最高级the most修饰时，关系代词通常只用that不用which。who用于指人，不填的情况只适用于关系代词作宾语时可省略，但这里作主语不能省略。解题技巧：定语从句中，先行词被最高级修饰时，关系代词用that；先行词既有人又有物时，也用that。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a green project called "Zero Waste Classroom". The project aims to help students understand the importance of environmental protection.

(8)_______, we were asked to bring reusable bottles instead of plastic ones. At first, many students found it (9)_______ to remember bringing their bottles every day. However, after two weeks, it became a good habit.

The most exciting part was the recycling competition. Each class had to collect waste paper and bottles. The class (10)_______ collected the most would win a prize. My classmates and I worked hard together. We put a special box at the back of the classroom and encouraged everyone to use it.

By the end of the month, we were surprised to find that we had produced 60% less waste than before. This experience taught us that small actions can make a big (11)_______. 

Now, the "Zero Waste" idea has spread to other schools in our city. We feel proud that we are helping to create a better (12)_______ for future generations.

questions.explanation: 这道题考查固定搭配。make a big difference是固定短语，意为"产生很大影响，意义重大"。句意：这次经历告诉我们，微小的行动也能产生巨大影响。make a decision（做决定）、make a mistake（犯错误）、make a problem（制造问题）都不符合语境。解题技巧：积累常见make搭配：make a difference（有影响）、make progress（取得进步）、make sense（有意义）、make sure（确保）。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a green project called "Zero Waste Classroom". The project aims to help students understand the importance of environmental protection.

(8)_______, we were asked to bring reusable bottles instead of plastic ones. At first, many students found it (9)_______ to remember bringing their bottles every day. However, after two weeks, it became a good habit.

The most exciting part was the recycling competition. Each class had to collect waste paper and bottles. The class (10)_______ collected the most would win a prize. My classmates and I worked hard together. We put a special box at the back of the classroom and encouraged everyone to use it.

By the end of the month, we were surprised to find that we had produced 60% less waste than before. This experience taught us that small actions can make a big (11)_______. 

Now, the "Zero Waste" idea has spread to other schools in our city. We feel proud that we are helping to create a better (12)_______ for future generations.

questions.explanation: 这道题考查词汇复现和主题理解。文章主题是环保项目（Zero Waste Classroom, environmental protection, less waste），因此这里应该是为未来世代创造更好的"环境"。school（学校）、classroom（教室）、competition（比赛）都太具体，不符合文章升华主题的需要。解题技巧：完形填空的最后一段通常升华主题，所选词汇应与文章主题（环保）一致，实现首尾呼应。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a green project called "Zero Waste Classroom". The project aims to help students understand the importance of environmental protection.

(8)_______, we were asked to bring reusable bottles instead of plastic ones. At first, many students found it (9)_______ to remember bringing their bottles every day. However, after two weeks, it became a good habit.

The most exciting part was the recycling competition. Each class had to collect waste paper and bottles. The class (10)_______ collected the most would win a prize. My classmates and I worked hard together. We put a special box at the back of the classroom and encouraged everyone to use it.

By the end of the month, we were surprised to find that we had produced 60% less waste than before. This experience taught us that small actions can make a big (11)_______. 

Now, the "Zero Waste" idea has spread to other schools in our city. We feel proud that we are helping to create a better (12)_______ for future generations.

questions.stem: According to the passage, what happens during sleep?
options: A. The brain stops working completely.
B. The brain processes information collected during the day.
C. The brain collects new information from books.
D. The brain becomes tired and weak.
questions.explanation: 这道题考查细节理解。根据文章第二段第一句"During sleep, the brain processes information collected during the day"可知，睡眠时大脑处理白天收集的信息。选项A（完全停止工作）与常识和原文相反；C（从书中收集新信息）错误，睡眠时不收集新信息；D（变得疲劳虚弱）未提及。解题技巧：细节题采用"定位法"，根据题干关键词during sleep找到第二段首句，对比选项和原文的同义表达（processes information）。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解
questions.group_passage:
Many students stay up late to study, thinking it will help them get better grades. However, recent studies show that sleep plays a crucial role in learning and memory.

During sleep, the brain processes information collected during the day. Scientists have found that people who get enough sleep can solve problems faster and remember things better. On the contrary, sleep-deprived students often have trouble concentrating in class.

Dr. Li, a sleep researcher, explains: "When you sleep, your brain is like a computer organizing files. It strengthens important memories and removes unnecessary information." He suggests that students should maintain a regular sleep schedule, even during exam periods.

Interestingly, taking short naps during the day can also improve learning efficiency. A 20-minute nap after lunch helps refresh the mind without causing drowsiness.

So, if you want to perform well in exams, going to bed early might be more effective than staying up all night to cram.

questions.stem: What does the underlined phrase "sleep-deprived" in Paragraph 2 probably mean?
questions.explanation: 这道题考查词义猜测。根据上下文逻辑，第二段先讲充足睡眠的好处（solve problems faster），然后用On the contrary（相反）转折，说明sleep-deprived的情况应该与"get enough sleep"相反，即"缺乏睡眠"。且后文"have trouble concentrating（难以集中注意力）"也是睡眠不足的表现。解题技巧：利用反义关系词（On the contrary）和上下文对比（get enough sleep vs sleep-deprived）猜测词义；deprived意为"被剥夺的"，sleep-deprived即"睡眠被剥夺的"。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测
questions.group_passage:
Many students stay up late to study, thinking it will help them get better grades. However, recent studies show that sleep plays a crucial role in learning and memory.

During sleep, the brain processes information collected during the day. Scientists have found that people who get enough sleep can solve problems faster and remember things better. On the contrary, sleep-deprived students often have trouble concentrating in class.

Dr. Li, a sleep researcher, explains: "When you sleep, your brain is like a computer organizing files. It strengthens important memories and removes unnecessary information." He suggests that students should maintain a regular sleep schedule, even during exam periods.

Interestingly, taking short naps during the day can also improve learning efficiency. A 20-minute nap after lunch helps refresh the mind without causing drowsiness.

So, if you want to perform well in exams, going to bed early might be more effective than staying up all night to cram.

questions.stem: What can we infer from Dr. Li's words in Paragraph 3?
options: A. Computers need sleep to work properly.
B. All memories should be kept in the brain.
C. Sleep helps organize and strengthen memories.
D. Files are stored in different parts of the brain.
questions.explanation: 这道题考查推理判断。Dr. Li说："睡眠时大脑就像电脑整理文件，强化重要记忆并删除不必要的信息。"由此可推断睡眠有助于组织和巩固记忆。选项A把比喻当事实，错误；B与原文"removes unnecessary information"矛盾；D提到的"different parts"未提及。解题技巧：推理题要基于原文但不止于原文，避免过度推断（如A）和与原文矛盾（如B）。抓住关键词"organizing files, strengthens important memories"推断出organize and strengthen。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断
questions.group_passage:
Many students stay up late to study, thinking it will help them get better grades. However, recent studies show that sleep plays a crucial role in learning and memory.

During sleep, the brain processes information collected during the day. Scientists have found that people who get enough sleep can solve problems faster and remember things better. On the contrary, sleep-deprived students often have trouble concentrating in class.

Dr. Li, a sleep researcher, explains: "When you sleep, your brain is like a computer organizing files. It strengthens important memories and removes unnecessary information." He suggests that students should maintain a regular sleep schedule, even during exam periods.

Interestingly, taking short naps during the day can also improve learning efficiency. A 20-minute nap after lunch helps refresh the mind without causing drowsiness.

So, if you want to perform well in exams, going to bed early might be more effective than staying up all night to cram.

questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: A. How to Pass Exams Successfully
B. The Relationship Between Sleep and Learning
C. Why Computers Need to Organize Files
D. The Importance of Taking Naps
questions.explanation: 这道题考查主旨大意/最佳标题。文章主要讨论睡眠与学习记忆的关系，第一段提出睡眠对学习很重要，第二段讲睡眠时大脑如何处理信息，第三段专家解释，第四段讲小睡，最后总结早睡比熬夜更有效。选项A太宽泛；C只是比喻细节；D（小睡的重要性）只是第四段细节。B准确概括了全文核心：睡眠与学习的关系。解题技巧：最佳标题要覆盖全文主要内容（sleep + learning/memory），不能过大（A）或过小（C, D）；通常出现在首尾段主题句中。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意
questions.group_passage:
Many students stay up late to study, thinking it will help them get better grades. However, recent studies show that sleep plays a crucial role in learning and memory.

During sleep, the brain processes information collected during the day. Scientists have found that people who get enough sleep can solve problems faster and remember things better. On the contrary, sleep-deprived students often have trouble concentrating in class.

Dr. Li, a sleep researcher, explains: "When you sleep, your brain is like a computer organizing files. It strengthens important memories and removes unnecessary information." He suggests that students should maintain a regular sleep schedule, even during exam periods.

Interestingly, taking short naps during the day can also improve learning efficiency. A 20-minute nap after lunch helps refresh the mind without causing drowsiness.

So, if you want to perform well in exams, going to bed early might be more effective than staying up all night to cram.

options: A. Make sure this place is free from distractions like phones.
B. The first step is to set realistic goals.
C. In addition, try to read with others.
D. However, reading is always difficult for beginners.
E. Also, choose books that match your interests.
questions.explanation: 这道题考查段落衔接和逻辑关系。空格后说"You don't have to finish a thick book in one day. Start with just 15 minutes...（不必一天读完厚书，从15分钟开始）"，这是在说设定合理目标，与B选项"第一步是设定现实目标"衔接自然。D选项（However）表示转折，与后文解释说明关系不符；其他选项内容与此处"从小目标开始"的主题不符。解题技巧：关注段落内部逻辑，后文的举例（15分钟）是对前文的解释说明，因此前文应是概括性观点（set realistic goals）。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Reading is an important part of our daily life. It not only brings us knowledge but also opens up a whole new world. However, many people struggle to develop a good reading habit.

(17)_______ You don't have to finish a thick book in one day. Start with just 15 minutes of reading before bed. Once you get used to it, you can gradually increase the time.

(18)_______ If you love stories, novels are perfect for you. If you prefer facts, try non-fiction books about history or science. Reading what interests you makes the activity enjoyable rather than boring.

Another tip is to create a comfortable reading environment. Find a quiet corner with good lighting. (19)_______ This helps your brain associate that place with reading.

(20)_______ Share what you've read with friends or join a book club. Discussing books with others deepens your understanding and makes reading more fun.

options: A. Make sure this place is free from distractions like phones.
B. The first step is to set realistic goals.
C. In addition, try to read with others.
D. However, reading is always difficult for beginners.
E. Also, choose books that match your interests.
questions.explanation: 这道题考查段落主题句。空格后说"If you love stories, novels are perfect... If you prefer facts, try non-fiction...（如果你喜欢故事，小说很合适；如果你喜欢事实，读非虚构类）"，这是在建议选择感兴趣的书，与E选项"Also, choose books that match your interests（此外，选择符合兴趣的书）"完全对应。Also表示并列补充，承接上文的第一个建议。A讲环境，C讲和他人一起读，都不符合此处"选书兴趣"的主题。解题技巧：段落主题句通常概括整段内容，后文的具体举例（stories vs facts）是对主题句的展开说明。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Reading is an important part of our daily life. It not only brings us knowledge but also opens up a whole new world. However, many people struggle to develop a good reading habit.

(17)_______ You don't have to finish a thick book in one day. Start with just 15 minutes of reading before bed. Once you get used to it, you can gradually increase the time.

(18)_______ If you love stories, novels are perfect for you. If you prefer facts, try non-fiction books about history or science. Reading what interests you makes the activity enjoyable rather than boring.

Another tip is to create a comfortable reading environment. Find a quiet corner with good lighting. (19)_______ This helps your brain associate that place with reading.

(20)_______ Share what you've read with friends or join a book club. Discussing books with others deepens your understanding and makes reading more fun.

options: A. Make sure this place is free from distractions like phones.
B. The first step is to set realistic goals.
C. In addition, try to read with others.
D. However, reading is always difficult for beginners.
E. Also, choose books that match your interests.
questions.explanation: 这道题考查代词指代和逻辑衔接。空格前说"Find a quiet corner with good lighting（找个光线好的安静角落）"，接着应该继续讲这个环境的要求，A选项"Make sure this place is free from distractions（确保这个地方没有干扰）"中的this place指代前文的corner，衔接紧密。后文"This helps your brain associate that place with reading"中的that place也印证了前文提到了具体的place。解题技巧：关注代词指代（this place, that place）来确定句子衔接，保持话题一致性（reading environment → place）。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Reading is an important part of our daily life. It not only brings us knowledge but also opens up a whole new world. However, many people struggle to develop a good reading habit.

(17)_______ You don't have to finish a thick book in one day. Start with just 15 minutes of reading before bed. Once you get used to it, you can gradually increase the time.

(18)_______ If you love stories, novels are perfect for you. If you prefer facts, try non-fiction books about history or science. Reading what interests you makes the activity enjoyable rather than boring.

Another tip is to create a comfortable reading environment. Find a quiet corner with good lighting. (19)_______ This helps your brain associate that place with reading.

(20)_______ Share what you've read with friends or join a book club. Discussing books with others deepens your understanding and makes reading more fun.

options: A. Make sure this place is free from distractions like phones.
B. The first step is to set realistic goals.
C. In addition, try to read with others.
D. However, reading is always difficult for beginners.
E. Also, choose books that match your interests.
questions.explanation: 这道题考查段落主题句和逻辑关系。空格后说"Share what you've read with friends or join a book club（和朋友分享或加入读书俱乐部）"，这是关于"和他人一起阅读"的建议，与C选项"In addition, try to read with others（此外，尝试和他人一起阅读）"对应。In addition表示补充并列，符合文章列举建议的结构（First... Also... Another... In addition...）。解题技巧：根据后文的具体行动（share with friends, book club, discussing with others）反推主题句应该是"和他人一起阅读"。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Reading is an important part of our daily life. It not only brings us knowledge but also opens up a whole new world. However, many people struggle to develop a good reading habit.

(17)_______ You don't have to finish a thick book in one day. Start with just 15 minutes of reading before bed. Once you get used to it, you can gradually increase the time.

(18)_______ If you love stories, novels are perfect for you. If you prefer facts, try non-fiction books about history or science. Reading what interests you makes the activity enjoyable rather than boring.

Another tip is to create a comfortable reading environment. Find a quiet corner with good lighting. (19)_______ This helps your brain associate that place with reading.

(20)_______ Share what you've read with friends or join a book club. Discussing books with others deepens your understanding and makes reading more fun.
